items.title: Why sustainable alternatives to plastic are struggling to compete
items.title_original: Why sustainable alternatives to plastic are struggling to compete
items.summary: Plastic pollution is choking the ocean, but sustainable alternatives - including seaweed - remain held back by tariffs, fragmented regulations and the overwhelming market advantage enjoyed by fossil fuel-based plastics.

items.title: Security Council LIVE: UN officials warn humanitarian toll in Ukraine is worsening
items.title_original: Security Council LIVE: UN officials warn humanitarian toll in Ukraine is worsening
items.summary: The Security Council met on Monday amid a sharp escalation in hostilities across Ukraine, where UN officials warned that the war has reached its deadliest point since Russia’s full-scale invasion in 2022. Briefing members, Rosemary DiCarlo, Under-Secretary-General for political affairs, said recent months had seen some of the most extensive aerial attacks of the conflict, while the humanitarian toll on civilians continued to mount on both sides of the front line.

items.title: Children are dying as US sanctions push Cuba to the brink, warns UN human rights chief
items.title_original: Children are dying as US sanctions push Cuba to the brink, warns UN human rights chief
items.summary: Children are dying because doctors cannot access essential medicines, UN human rights chief Volker Türk said in a stark warning on Monday, calling for the immediate lifting of United States sanctions against the Caribbean nation that were causing “widespread harm”.

items.title: Marking historic progress on rights for persons with disabilities, UN conference tackles critical gaps
items.title_original: Marking historic progress on rights for persons with disabilities, UN conference tackles critical gaps
items.summary: Historical gains have been hard-won, but much more needs to be done to advance progress in realising promises made two decades ago, said the UN chief at the opening on Tuesday of the 19th global meeting on the rights of persons with disabilities at UN Headquarters.

items.summary_original: Urban development, green spaces, and maritime activity converge in this Northern California city.
items.contentExcerpt: Urban development, green spaces, and maritime activity converge in this Northern California city. A period of unsettled weather brought scattered showers and thunderstorms to California’s Bay Area on May 27, 2026. That afternoon, a break in the clouds left downtown San Francisco and nearby communities beneath mostly cloud-free skies, allowing an astronaut aboard the International Space Station to take this photograph. The image captures two of the region's iconic bridges. The Golden Gate Brid...

items.contentExcerpt: “三北”工程河西走廊－塔克拉玛干沙漠边缘阻击战现场推进会9日在新疆维吾尔自治区巴音郭楞蒙古自治州召开。中共中央政治局委员、国务院副总理何立峰出席会议并讲话，他强调，要深入学习贯彻习近平生态文明思想和习近平总书记重要指示批示精神，牢固树立和践行正确政绩观，出实招、求实效、重实绩，全力打好“三北”工程西部片区阻击战，推动东中西部三大标志性战役如期取得阶段性重要成果。
article_keywords: 工程西部阻击战片区现场推

items.title: Afghanistan faces ‘lost generation of talent and potential,’ Security Council hears
items.title_original: Afghanistan faces ‘lost generation of talent and potential,’ Security Council hears
items.summary: As the Security Council met on Afghanistan, senior officials and civil society representatives delivered a clear warning Monday: despite relative security under the Taliban, worsening humanitarian conditions, restrictions on women and growing economic pressures are creating a fragile and uncertain future.

items.title: Flight Dynamics Research Facility Characteristics
items.title_original: Flight Dynamics Research Facility Characteristics
items.summary: HomeCharacteristics The Flight Dynamics Research Facility (FDRF) is a large, subsonic wind tunnel with a vertical test section for conducting flight dynamics research for stability, controllability, free-fall and aircraft spin, and spin recovery testing of atmospheric vehicles. Characteristics Flight Dynamics Flight Research Aerosciences Evaluation and Test Capabilities

items.title: NASA Marches Toward Artemis III Mission in 2027, Names Crew Members
items.title_original: NASA Marches Toward Artemis III Mission in 2027, Names Crew Members
items.summary: Taking another step toward one of the most complex human spaceflight missions in recent history, NASA on Tuesday provided new Artemis III details and announced the four prime crew members and a backup for the test flight. The mission will undertake a series of challenging tests in Earth orbit in 2027, essential for Artemis IV, the […]

items.contentExcerpt: Taking another step toward one of the most complex human spaceflight missions in recent history, NASA on Tuesday provided new Artemis III details and announced the four prime crew members and a backup for the test flight. The mission will undertake a series of challenging tests in Earth orbit in 2027, essential for Artemis IV, the first planned crewed mission to the lunar South Pole in 2028. During Artemis III, the agency’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket will launch the Orion spacecraft and...

items.title: NASA Knows: What Is Mass Distribution?
items.title_original: NASA Knows: What Is Mass Distribution?
items.summary: This article is for students grades 5-8. Mass distribution affects everything from galaxy shapes to aircraft design to planetary rotation. It’s used to map stars in our universe, figure out what planets are made of, and even to determine how luggage is loaded onto an airplane. Mass distribution can be a tricky thing to understand. […]
items.summary_original: This article is for students grades 5-8. Mass distribution affects everything from galaxy shapes to aircraft design to planetary rotation. It’s used to map stars in our universe, figure out what planets are made of, and even to determine how luggage is loaded onto an airplane. Mass distribution can be a tricky thing to understand. […]
items.contentExcerpt: This article is for students grades 5-8. Mass distribution affects everything from galaxy shapes to aircraft design to planetary rotation. It’s used to map stars in our universe, figure out what planets are made of, and even to determine how luggage is loaded onto an airplane. Mass distribution can be a tricky thing to understand. So, let’s explore it using an everyday example: a soccer ball. Have you ever kicked a soccer ball and wondered why it curves, spins, or sometimes wobbles? Mass dist...

items.title: Fresh strikes on Tyre kill eight, as UN puts Lebanon destruction bill at $365 million, and rising
items.title_original: Fresh strikes on Tyre kill eight, as UN puts Lebanon destruction bill at $365 million, and rising
items.summary: Lebanon has suffered more than $365 million in damage to buildings across Beirut and Mount Lebanon since the latest escalation, according to a new UN-led assessment released on Tuesday, as fresh strikes in Tyre underscored the fragility of a ceasefire that has failed to stop the fighting.

items.title: Palestinians face systematic abuse by Israeli settlers and Hamas alike: Independent investigators
items.title_original: Palestinians face systematic abuse by Israeli settlers and Hamas alike: Independent investigators
items.summary: Palestinian civilians are trapped between escalating settler violence in the occupied West Bank and fear-based Hamas rule in the Gaza Strip, investigators appointed by the UN Human Rights Council said on Tuesday in a new report.

items.title: Security Council LIVE: Central Africa in the spotlight, including outlawed Lord's Resistance Army
items.title_original: Security Council LIVE: Central Africa in the spotlight, including outlawed Lord's Resistance Army
items.summary: The Security Council meets this morning to discuss the situation in Central Africa, including efforts to combat the so-called Lord’s Resistance Army (LRA), one of the region’s longest-running security threats, responsible for over 100,000 deaths, the UN estimates. Ambassadors have been briefed by the regional office (UNOCA) and its strategy for improving civilian protection, humanitarian access and cross-border cooperation.

items.title: ‘Rare, untreatable strain’: Ebola toll mounts in eastern DR Congo
items.title_original: ‘Rare, untreatable strain’: Ebola toll mounts in eastern DR Congo
items.summary: The top UN aid official in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is in Ituri province – the epicentre of the country's Ebola outbreak – for a three-day assessment visit, as the confirmed case count reaches 515 across three eastern provinces.
items.summary_original: The top UN aid official in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is in Ituri province – the epicentre of the country's Ebola outbreak – for a three-day assessment visit, as the confirmed case count reaches 515 across three eastern provinces.

items.title: Artemis III Crew Announced
items.title_original: Artemis III Crew Announced
items.summary: NASA astronaut Andre Douglas, ESA (European Space Agency) astronaut Luca Parmitano, and NASA astronauts Randy Bresnik and Frank Rubio take a photo together on June 9, 2026. The four were announced as the Artemis III crew. NASA’s Artemis III mission in low Earth orbit will test integrated operations between the Orion spacecraft and one or […]
items.summary_original: NASA astronaut Andre Douglas, ESA (European Space Agency) astronaut Luca Parmitano, and NASA astronauts Randy Bresnik and Frank Rubio take a photo together on June 9, 2026. The four were announced as the Artemis III crew. NASA’s Artemis III mission in low Earth orbit will test integrated operations between the Orion spacecraft and one or […]
items.contentExcerpt: NASA astronaut Andre Douglas, ESA (European Space Agency) astronaut Luca Parmitano, and NASA astronauts Randy Bresnik and Frank Rubio take a photo together on June 9, 2026. The four were announced as the Artemis III crew. NASA’s Artemis III mission in low Earth orbit will test integrated operations between the Orion spacecraft and one or both commercial landers from SpaceX and Blue Origin respectively. Learn more about the next Artemis mission and the crew.

items.title: NASA’s INCUS Satellites Progress Toward Launch
items.title_original: NASA’s INCUS Satellites Progress Toward Launch
items.summary: Description One of the three satellites that make up NASA’s INCUS (Investigation of Convective Updrafts) mission sits on a fixture at the facilities of Blue Canyon Technologies in Lafayette, Colorado. The satellite completed testing in preparation for launch in late May 2026. The mission will make the first space-based survey of the dynamics of tropical […]

The satellite completed testing in preparation for launch in late May 2026. The mission will make the first space-based survey of the dynamics of tropical […]
items.contentExcerpt: One of the three satellites that make up NASA’s INCUS (Investigation of Convective Updrafts) mission sits on a fixture at the facilities of Blue Canyon Technologies in Lafayette, Colorado. The satellite completed testing in preparation for launch in late May 2026. The mission will make the first space-based survey of the dynamics of tropical convective storms. The three nearly identical satellites will fly in tight coordination in low Earth orbit, with the first and second satellites separate...
